close_reason: Vec for 2 fixed elements: marginal optimization, not worth the complexity
description:
src/lib.rs:198 'sources: Vec<Arc<dyn TokenCredential>>' is constructed exactly once with two elements at line 189, then iterated read-only thereafter. Vec carries len, cap, and a heap pointer for what is structurally [Arc<dyn TokenCredential>; 2].

Idiomatic alternatives:
1. Box<[Arc<dyn TokenCredential>]> — same heap allocation but no spare capacity, no resize machinery.
2. SmallVec<[..; 2]> — stack-only for the common case.
3. Two separate fields (primary: Arc<...>, fallback: Option<Arc<...>>) — encodes the 'two-tier' semantics in the type.

Why it matters (engineering basis): currently the code admits a future bug where a contributor adds a third source. The 'try in order, return first success' loop will continue to work, but the silent-fallback risk (see crate-secretsx-bdu0.7) compounds with every added source. Encoding the 2-source assumption in the type prevents this drift.

Marked opinion because the engineering basis is real but the cost of the current Vec is essentially zero.
title: ChainedCredential.sources: Vec for 2 fixed elements; smallvec or [_;2]
